title: Multi-Column Layout
codeExample:
Multi-Column Layout Example

CSS multi-column layout allows content to flow into multiple columns, similar to a newspaper or magazine. This is ideal for long-form reading content.

The browser automatically balances the content across columns. You can control the number of columns, gap between them, and even add rules (lines) between columns.

Use column-span: all to make headings or images span across all columns. Multi-column layout is great for articles, blogs, and text-heavy content.

For card layouts or more complex designs, consider using CSS Grid or Flexbox instead. Multi-column is specifically designed for text flow.

quiz.question: Which property makes an element span all columns?
